violations.text: Food was observed held at 53-56 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit for about 2-3 hours. Food was relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool down. The ambient air temperature was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Prep unit must be adjusted/repaired/replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the walk-in cooler in covered containers. Covers were removed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
Salami was observed held past 7 days. Salami was date marked 4/27. Salami was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2023
